question: For no torsion, the plane of bending should
options: be parallel to one of the principal axes
pass through shear centre of section
pass through neutral axis of the section
pass through centre of gravity of the section
explanation: For no torsion, the plane of bending should be parallel to one of the principal axes.
subject: Torsion
ai_correct: The shear centre is the point through which a transverse load must pass to cause bending without any twisting (torsion) of the beam section.
ai_wrong: Students often confuse the shear centre with the centroid or the neutral axis. While they may coincide in symmetric sections, they are distinct concepts.
formula: Torsion = 0 if Load passes through Shear Centre
